title: Wiring the Swarm channel via ReactorBuilder.withSync() avoids monkey-patching the reactor.
content:
## Claim
In `createBrowserReactor()`, the Swarm channel is wired through `ReactorBuilder.withSync(syncBuilder)` rather than `withChannelScheme(...)`. This avoids monkey-patching or SQL hacks.

## Evidence
From the source: "The ReactorBuilder has two code paths: 1) `if (this.channelScheme)` — auto-creates a single factory type; 2) `else if (this.syncBuilder)` — uses YOUR factory. By using path 2, we get full control over which channel types are available."

The build-time registration looks like:

```typescript
const { syncBuilder, registerGqlFactory } = createSwarmSyncBuilder(logger, jwtHandler);
new ReactorBuilder().withSync(syncBuilder).withJwtHandler(jwtHandler);
```

## Consequences
- Swarm remotes persist in `sync_remotes` and are recreated natively on startup.
- No dynamic re-registration or private-API reach-ins.
- Rejects hacks: "No monkey-patching. No SQL hacks. No dynamic re-registration."

## Source
`connect/src/utils/reactor.ts`; Swarm Channel Architecture, "Wiring Into createBrowserReactor".
description: ReactorBuilder has two code paths; using the syncBuilder path instead of channelScheme gives full control over channel types without patching.
